codeReview.summary: Reviewed prior failed features (transactions-table @ e099bd3, mobile-responsive @ e798bba, animations-realtime-polish @ aa42b5d), their prior scrutiny reports, and the fix commit 6a353aa. The fix resolves all three blocking findings: Address column no longer uses trade IDs and now reads participant-address fields with a '—' fallback when absent; mobile hamburger-menu Connect Wallet control enforces a 44px minimum button height; and the chart now subscribes to realtime trades and appends WS trade prices to the series while retaining REST candle polling fallback when WS is unavailable.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature metadata still uses `frontend-ui-worker`, but workers continue deviating to `frontend-worker` at runtime.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission features.json uses `skillName: frontend-ui-worker` for this milestone (e.g., lines 516, 555, 590, 639), while fix handoff `.../handoffs/2026-03-31T13-27-23-592Z__fix-final-scrutiny-issues__fa867cf3-6efc-4101-a4c4-cd892abdff95.json` records a deviation to `frontend-worker`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Trade contracts/API still do not expose participant wallet addresses, so Address can only render '—' unless upstream schemas are extended.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: shared/src/index.ts `Trade` includes maker/taker order IDs but no wallet address fields (lines 48-55); api/src/routes/trades.ts maps response to `{ id, price, quantity, side, timestamp }` only.

summary: Pass. The rerun fix feature adequately addresses each of the three original blocking scrutiny failures across table address sourcing, mobile wallet-button touch target sizing, and WebSocket-driven chart updates with REST fallback preserved.
